Papua New Guinea (modern borders) — cane's ancestral origin
description: Modern political border (post-1975 independence). ORIGIN of Saccharum officinarum — domesticated here ~10,000 years ago and spread across the Pacific via Austronesian migrations long before reaching India, Persia, or Europe.

Brazil (modern borders) — largest cane producer today + enslaved-labour history
description: Modern political border. 39% of world cane sugar — São Paulo and NE plantation belt. Brazil received ~40% of ALL enslaved Africans shipped across the Atlantic (Eltis & Richardson 2010). Abolition was 1888, last country in the Americas.

India (modern borders) — 20% of world production + original crystallization
description: Modern political border (post-1947). India developed first crystallized sugar ~500 BCE. Today: 20% of world production, Uttar Pradesh + Maharashtra are anchors.

Caribbean (modern borders) — historical 'sugar islands' of the triangle trade
description: Modern political borders. The 'sugar islands' — Cuba, Hispaniola (Haiti + DR), Jamaica, Barbados — formed the core of the Atlantic slave-trade triangle. Haiti (Saint-Domingue) was called 'the richest colony on earth' in 1788 — wealth built entirely on enslaved labour.
